## Emergency Preparedness Tips from the Red Cross

The Red Cross can help you prepare your family, your pets and your home for common emergencies and natural disasters. Read below for information about [**emergencies that can occur where you live**,](https://www.redcross.org/get-help/how-to-prepare-for-emergencies/common-natural-disasters-across-us.html) and learn the difference between a "watch" and a "warning". Get tips for preparedness steps you can take today, what to do in an emergency, and how to repair and rebuild afterward.

![ERV icon](https://www.redcross.org/content/dam/icons/disaster-services/erv/ERV-1000x1000-G-RC.png.transform/282/q70/feature/image.png)

##### **Need Help Now?**

If you are in immediate need of help, please [**contact your local Red Cross »**](https://www.redcross.org/find-your-local-chapter.html) or [**find an open shelter »**](https://www.redcross.org/get-help/disaster-relief-and-recovery-services/find-an-open-shelter.html)

![ERV icon](https://www.redcross.org/content/dam/icons/general-icons/house/House-1000x1000-RC.png.transform/282/q70/feature/image.png)

##### **Disasters Where You Live**

Some disasters can occur anywhere. Others are more common in specific parts of the country. **[See which disasters are most important for _you_ to prepare for](https://www.redcross.org/get-help/how-to-prepare-for-emergencies/common-natural-disasters-across-us.html).**

### This Month's Common Emergencies

It's April, which means it's time to be prepared for tornado, flood, and home fire.

### Prepare Your Home and Family for 25 Different Emergencies

From chemical spills to winter storms, the Red Cross has easy to follow tips for preparing your family - as well as for how to stay safe during and after the emergency.
